doc_title: Dance Any Beat: Blending Beats with Visuals in Dance Video Generation

items.fact: Early research [43, 69, 9] focused on producing 2D dance sequences
items.evidence_span: Early research [43, 69, 9] focused on producing $2 \mathrm{D}$ dance sequences

items.fact: GANs [25, 48, 10, 21] are used for 3D motion generation
Transformers [17, 27, 47, 35, 64] are used for 3D motion generation
AIST++ dataset [27] has significantly advanced the field
FACT [27] uses a full-attention-based cross-modal transformer
Bailando [47] combines a pose VQ-VAE with a Motion GPT
Ren et al. [37] synthesize videos using motion sequences
Wang et al. [54] developed a conditional GAN architecture
Yang et al. [62] introduced a pose-guided approach
Wang et al. [55] unveiled the Latent Image Animator (LIA)
Existing methods [26, 27, 47, 35] focus on generating joint sequences
items.evidence_span: Existing methods [26, 27, 47, 35] focus on generating joint sequences

items.fact: conditional image-to-video (I2V) generation guided by textual descriptions [54, 39, 11]
Librosa [30] for audio signal analysis and beat extraction
MM-Diffusion [40] is the leading model in unconditional video generation
items.evidence_span: We benchmark our baseline model DabFusion ${ }_{b}$ and enhanced model DabFusion ${ }_{e}$ against MM-Diffusion [40], the leading model in unconditional video generation.
